content_markdown:
# A Daily Grind of Faithfulness

## Scripture

"So we fix our eyes not on what is seen, but on what is unseen, since what is seen is temporary, but what is unseen is eternal." - 2 Corinthians 4:18 (NIV)

## Reflection

Just as a pour-over requires patience and attention to detail, our faith walks require dedication and focus on the unseen realities of God's Kingdom. We must be willing to let go of the temporary and fleeting things of this world, and instead, fix our eyes on the eternal purposes of God. The process of extraction, where the coffee grounds release their flavors and oils, reminds us that our spiritual growth often requires the gentle removal of the old to make room for the new. Just as the roasting process transforms the raw beans into a rich and complex brew, our trials and hardships can refine us, bringing out the depth of our character and the beauty of our faith.

## Prayer

We ask that you would help us to fix our eyes on the eternal realities of your Kingdom, and that we would be faithful in the daily grind of our spiritual walks.

## One-Line Application

As you face the challenges of your day, focus on what is unseen and eternal, rather than the temporary and fleeting.
